content:
Starting a new thread to discuss upstreaming an abstraction for xarray - original patch here .

This is semi-related to Read/black tree deprecation, since we plan use xarray instead of rbtree for a use case in binder.

Obligatory thanks to @Asahi Lina who authored the original patch :clap:

@Miguel Ojeda, @Wedson Almeida Filho and I met today and came up with the following (tentative) plan:

  1. Start a new patch set with a minimal implementation. So the first commit will expose only new, replace, set, get, and remove.
  2. Add get_mut along with GuardMut (mutable version of Guard).

  3. Add find and find_mut. Find is similar to get, but returns the next larger index if there is no value at the provided index. We need this for binder.

    3a. Here we may try to encode the invariant that a lock must be acquired via xa_lock, for certain operations (get/find so far), into the type system.

  4. Add the more interesting stuff that @Asahi Lina needs: alloc, alloc_limits, reserve, and reserve_limits.

Perhaps 3a should just go with 1, even though there will only be 1 function get that uses it at that point in the series.

I'll link a GH branch in the coming days. Any other thoughts? (cc: @Alice Ryhl )

p.s. for reference, quote from: https://docs.kernel.org/core-api/xarray.html#locking:
"If you want to take advantage of the lock to protect the data structures that you are storing in the XArray, you can call xa_lock() before calling xa_load(), then take a reference count on the object you have found before calling xa_unlock(). This will prevent stores from removing the object from the array between looking up the object and incrementing the refcount. You can also use RCU to avoid dereferencing freed memory, but an explanation of that is beyond the scope of this document."
XArray shouldn't be movable? The new should return impl PinInit<Self> instead.

Here is a branch: https://github.com/matthewtgilbride/linux/commits/rust-next-xarray
The commits go:

  1. basics (new/replace/get/set/remove, also includes find)
  2. doctests - could be merged into 1, but I wanted to leave @Asahi Lina 's original commit alone other than adding find.
  3. _mut variants for get and find
  4. reservation mechanism

@Miguel Ojeda , would it be possible to add the kunit doctest commits to rust-dev, and then maybe I could open a PR against that branch to facilitate some feedback before sending this series to the official mailing list?

Gary Guo said:

XArray shouldn't be movable? The new should return impl PinInit<Self> instead.

This flies over my head a bit...but I will happily follow whatever the right thing to do is.

Another naive question along the same lines....should set, replace, and remove also take self: Pin<&mut Self> instead of self: Pin<&Self>, or is the idea that entries can be added/deleted without exclusive access?

Matt Gilbride said:

Miguel Ojeda , would it be possible to add the kunit doctest commits to rust-dev, and then maybe I could open a PR against that branch to facilitate some feedback before sending this series to the official mailing list?

Yeah, definitely, we should have the KUnit series in rust-dev (in fact, that was the idea so that people could easily use it together with other upgrades like the toolchain one, since the KUnit Rust doctests will go through the KUnit tree).
(@Boqun Feng I may send a v2 with some small changes for KUnit, but I guess it is not a big deal if we put v1 already and then force push it later)
Sure, I will add it (as it is) now
@Matt Gilbride one tiny thing, I also have the bindgen 0.65.1 commit in the rust-dev, would that be a problem to you? I can drop it if you prefer, not a big deal for me.

Boqun Feng said:

Matt Gilbride one tiny thing, I also have the bindgen 0.65.1 commit in the rust-dev, would that be a problem to you? I can drop it if you prefer, not a big deal for me.

Pushed out with the bindgen 0.65.1 commit, I can always drop that later if that's a problem

Boqun Feng said:

Matt Gilbride one tiny thing, I also have the bindgen 0.65.1 commit in the rust-dev, would that be a problem to you? I can drop it if you prefer, not a big deal for me.

This is fine for me - one thing that tripped me up is that its cargo install bindgen-cli instead of bindgen as of v0.61.0. I added a commit to the base of my PR here for that: https://github.com/Rust-for-Linux/linux/pull/1019

I wrote up a draft message about migrating from rbtree to xarray in binder here: https://github.com/matthewtgilbride/linux/pull/3/files

That's just a PR into my own repo for discussion purposes...let me now if there is a better way to gather feedback. Also - once I send it to the mailing list - is a link to a markdown file on GH (or alternatively a public google doc link) appropriate?

Just looking at this - FYI, for commits where I'm the primary author (in Git), you need to keep my signoff. If you want to submit them yourself, you can add your own signoff at the end (or if you list yourself as Co-developed-by, BTW I think Co-Authored-by is incorrect).

Asahi Lina said:

Just looking at this - FYI, for commits where I'm the primary author (in Git), you need to keep my signoff. If you want to submit them yourself, you can add your own signoff at the end (or if you list yourself as Co-developed-by, BTW I think Co-Authored-by is incorrect).

Sure thing. I'm still quite new to all the mailing list stuff so I am happy to do whatever credits you properly for the original authorship. The commits in that PR are quite a mess at the moment, but the tip of that tree represents the minimal surface that we think we need in binder. I'm trying out using them there today.
